title: Coherent Diffraction Imaging (CDI) — lensless phase retrieval
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (genesis fan-out artifact). Physics below is extracted from mine_example/lensless.md and canonical CDI literature (Fienup, Miao, Elser).

E.description: A coherent beam illuminates an isolated object in free space; the far-field detector records the squared magnitude of its Fourier transform. Image recovery is the phase-retrieval problem: given only |F{O}|, recover O using a finite-support constraint (oversampling sigma >= 2 per dim) and non-negativity / atomicity priors.
E.forward_model: I(k) = |F{O(r)}|^2 + n(k),  O(r) supported on S subset Omega
E.world_state_x: Complex object O(r) in C^{H x W} with compact support S (isolated specimen)
E.observation_y: Far-field diffraction pattern I(k) in R^{K x K} (non-negative, Poisson)
physical_parameters_theta: wavelength lambda (X-ray typical: 0.1-2 nm; optical: 400-800 nm)
detector pixel size dx; oversampling sigma = (wavelength * z) / (dx * L_object)
support mask S (estimated via shrinkwrap or provided as prior)
beam stop region (masked center of detector)

W.regime: Strongly non-convex phaseless recovery. Hayes ambiguity (global phase, conjugate flip, sub-pixel translation) resolved by support compactness. Oversampling sigma >= 2 in each dimension is necessary; beam-stop inpainting and partial coherence further degrade conditioning.

C.solver_class: projection iterations (HIO, ER, RAAR, CF), Shrinkwrap, ADMM-CDI, Wirtinger-flow, learned (PRCGAN, CDI-Net)
C.error_bound: ||O_hat - O||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(photon_count) + C2 * (1 - sigma_oversample / 2); R-factor convergence is the empirical stopping rule
